message:
fix: migrate gt mail to use bd v0.32.0 issue commands

bd v0.32.0 removed mail commands. Updated gt mail to use:
- bd list --type message (inbox)
- bd show (read)
- bd close (delete/ack)
- bd create --type message (send)

Sender/thread/reply-to now stored in labels and extracted on read.
Added --pinned flag (blocked by bd pin bug gt-zr0a).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-70b3","title":"detectSender() doesn't recognize crew workers","description":"## Problem

`detectSender()` in mail.go only detects polecats, not crew workers.

## Current Code (line 445)

```go
// If in a rig's polecats directory, extract address
if strings.Contains(cwd, "/polecats/") {
    // extract rig/polecat
}

// Default to mayor
return "mayor/"
```

## Symptom

Emma (crew worker at `/Users/stevey/gt/beads/crew/emma`) runs:
- `gt mail inbox` → checks `mayor/` inbox (wrong!)
- Should check `beads/emma` or `beads/crew/emma`

## Fix

Add crew detection:
```go
// If in a rig's crew directory, extract address  
if strings.Contains(cwd, "/crew/") {
    parts := strings.Split(cwd, "/crew/")
    if len(parts) >= 2 {
        rigPath := parts[0]
        crewName := strings.Split(parts[1], "/")[0]
        rigName := filepath.Base(rigPath)
        return fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", rigName, crewName)
    }
}
```

## Also Check

- `gt prime` correctly detects role, so there may be another detection function that works
- Should unify detection logic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:40:26.520559-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:55:54.679969-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T21:55:54.679969-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-70b3","depends_on_id":"gt-l4gm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:50:04.812663-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-71i","title":"Update architecture.md: Engineer role and Beads merge queue","description":"Update docs/architecture.md with recent design decisions:

2. Merge Queue section: Document Beads-native model

3. CLI section: Add gt mq commands

4. Key Design Decisions: Add decisions for:

   - #16: Engineer role (distinct from Refinery place)

 {"id":"gt-bjft","title":"gt spawn should auto-start refinery/witness if not running","description":"When spawning a polecat, the infrastructure (refinery, witness) should already be running.

## Current Behavior
spawn.go only:1. Assigns issue to polecat2. Sends work mail3. Starts polecat session

The refinery and witness must be started separately.

## Expected Behavior (per user feedback)
When spawning a polecat, if the rig's refinery or witness is not running, auto-start them.

## Options

### Option A: spawn auto-starts infrastructure
Check if refinery/witness running before spawn, start if not.

### Option B: gt swarm start <rig> command
Explicit command that:
1. Starts refinery
2. Starts witness
3. Optionally spawns polecats on bd ready issues

### Option C: gt rig start <rig>
Similar to Option B but as rig lifecycle command.

## Related
- gt-n7z7: refinery --foreground race condition bug
- gt-u1j.18: witness CLI commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:58:10.146332-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:58:10.146332-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-bmjw","title":"gt polecat add: should handle existing branch gracefully","description":"## Problem

`gt polecat add gastown Nux` fails if the branch `polecat/Nux` already exists.

## Current Behavior

```
fatal: a branch named 'polecat/Nux' already exists
```

## Expected Behavior

Should either:
1. Reuse the existing branch
2. Or prompt to delete/recreate
3. Or auto-suffix: polecat/Nux-2

## Context

Branch may exist from previous polecat that was removed but branch wasn't cleaned up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:52:09.361672-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:08:48.858604-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:08:48.858604-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 8b659de"}
+{"id":"gt-bnik","title":"gt nudge should accept partial/fuzzy session names","description":"Currently gt nudge requires the exact tmux session name (e.g., gt-gastown-crew-max). Should be more forgiving:

1. Accept partial matches when unambiguous (e.g., 'max' → gt-gastown-crew-max)
2. Accept shorthand like 'gastown/max' or 'crew/max'
3. Show helpful error with suggestions when ambiguous

Examples that should work:
- gt nudge max '...' → matches gt-gastown-crew-max
- gt nudge gastown/max '...' → matches gt-gastown-crew-max
- gt nudge beads-dave '...' → matches gt-beads-crew-dave","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:53:44.834337-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:53:44.834337-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-bqbw","title":"detectSender() doesn't recognize crew workers","description":"## Problem

detectSender() in internal/cmd/mail.go only checks for /polecats/ directories. Crew workers in /crew/<name>/ fall through to the default 'mayor/', so:
- gt mail inbox shows mayor's inbox instead of the crew worker's
- gt mail send sets the wrong From address

## Fix

Add crew worker detection before the /polecats/ check:

if strings.Contains(cwd, "/crew/") {
    parts := strings.Split(cwd, "/crew/")
    ...
    return fmt.Sprintf("%s/crew/%s", rigName, crewMember)
}

## Affected
- Any crew worker running gt mail inbox without explicit address
- Crew worker handoffs (wrong sender)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T20:09:42.556373-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.861756-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.861756-08:00"}

Likely a schema migration issue - bd schema has 'pinned' field but SQLite table doesn't.

## Reproduction
gt mail send gastown/Immortan -s 'test' -m 'test'

## Error
table issues has no column named pinned

## Fix
Either:
1. Add migration to add pinned column
2. Remove pinned from insert if not present
3. Regenerate DB from JSONL","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:53:09.262403-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.553097-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:17:03.637686-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-bzd","title":"beads: Stop searching upward when .beads found","description":"## Problem

When running bd commands in a nested directory structure with multiple .beads directories, bd shows a confusing warning:

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ WARNING: 2 beads databases detected in directory hierarchy              ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║ Multiple databases can cause confusion and database pollution.          ║
║                                                                          ║
║   /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/.beads (261 issues)                           ║
║   /Users/stevey/gt/.beads (21 issues)                                    ║
║                                                                          ║
║ WARNING: Not using the closest database! Check your BEADS_DB setting.   ║
║                                                                          ║
║ RECOMMENDED: Consolidate or remove unused databases to avoid confusion. ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Why This Is Wrong

In Gas Town, nested .beads directories are **intentional and necessary**:
- Town level: /Users/stevey/gt/.beads (mail, town-level issues)
- Rig level: /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/.beads (gastown project issues)
- Worker level: polecats have their own beads in worktrees

These are **unrelated** beads instances for different scopes. They should never be consolidated.

## Expected Behavior

When bd finds a .beads directory, it should:
1. Use that directory (closest ancestor wins)
2. **Stop searching upward** - do not look for parent .beads directories
3. **No warning** about multiple databases

## Current Behavior

bd searches all the way up to root, finds all .beads directories, and warns about "multiple databases" even though they are separate, intentional instances.

## Fix

In the database discovery code, stop the upward search as soon as a .beads directory is found. The first .beads found is the one to use, and parent directories are out of scope.

## Note

This is a beads issue, filed here for tracking. Should be implemented in the beads codebase.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T19:09:44.295743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T00:44:28.741548-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T00:44:28.741548-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-frs","title":"Polecat name pooling: Bounded reusable names","description":"Polecats reuse names from a bounded pool (50) with overflow to sequence numbers.

## Naming Scheme
- Pool: polecat-01 through polecat-50 (prefer low numbers)
- Overflow: <rigname>-<sequenceNumber> (e.g., beads-51, gastown-52)

## Design
- Witness tracks which pool names are in use
- On spawn: pick first available from pool
- If pool exhausted: use rigname-N format
- On completion: pool name returns, sequence numbers don't

## Why?
- User experience: tmux sessions survive polecat restarts
- Users stay attached, see new polecat start (like mayor respawn loop)
- Bounded resource usage for common case
- Scales beyond 50 when needed

## Implementation
- Witness maintains name allocation in beads or local state
- Tmux session runs respawn loop (like mayor)
- Name released on graceful exit or when witness detects dead session","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.43866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:22:52.551244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:29:30.648439-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-g2d","title":"Mayor session cycling prompting","description":"Add session cycling section to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## When to Cycle

Cycle proactively when:
- Running for several hours
- Context feels crowded (losing track of earlier state)
- Major phase completed
- About to start complex new work

## Composing Handoff Notes

1. Gather information:
   town status    # Overall health
   town rigs      # Each rig state
   town inbox     # Pending messages
   bd ready       # Work items

2. Compose note with this structure:

[HANDOFF_TYPE]: mayor_cycle
[TIMESTAMP]: <current time>
[SESSION_DURATION]: <how long running>

## Active Swarms
<per-rig swarm status>

## Rig Status
<table of rig health>

## Pending Escalations
<issues needing your decision>

## In-Flight Decisions
<decisions being made>

## Recent Actions
<last 5-10 things you did>

## Delegated Work
<work sent to refineries>

## User Requests
<pending user asks>

## Next Steps
<what next session should do>

## Warnings/Notes
<critical info for next session>

3. Send handoff:
   town mail send mayor/ -s "Session Handoff" -m "<note>"

4. End session - next instance picks up from handoff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:26.188561-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:39.861022-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g2d","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.361163-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-g44u","title":"Molecule Workflow Engine: Composable Crystallized Workflows","description":"# Epic: Molecule Workflow Engine

**Vision**: Molecules are crystallized, composable, nondeterministic-idempotent workflow templates. Any worker can pick up where any other worker was interrupted and continue along the molecule.

**Christmas Target**: Full molecule-based workflow engine operational by Dec 25, 2025.

## The Core Concepts

1. **Molecule**: Read-only workflow template (beads issue with type=molecule)
2. **Atom/Step**: Individual work unit with prose instructions
3. **Bond**: Dependency between steps
4. **Polymer/Derived**: Molecule composed from other molecules
5. **Instance**: Concrete beads created when molecule is attached to work

## Key Features Needed

### 1. Molecule Composition (Includes Directive)
Molecules can include other molecules:
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: gastown-polecat
Includes: mol-engineer-in-box

## Step: install-binary
Build and install the local gt binary.
Needs: submit
\`\`\`

### 2. Standard Molecules
- mol-install-go-binary: Single step to build/install gt
- mol-gastown-polecat: engineer-in-box + install-binary

### 3. Spawn Integration
\`gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>\` creates molecule instance then starts polecat on first ready step.

### 4. Nondeterministic Idempotence
- Steps are atomic (pending → in_progress → completed)
- Any worker can pick up any ready step
- Step timeout/recovery for stuck workers

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Polecats can be spawned with mol-gastown-polecat
- [ ] Derived molecules work end-to-end
- [ ] 10+ polecat swarm completes molecule workflows
- [ ] install-go-binary step runs after successful merges","status":"closed","issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:49:32.005023-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:23:08.857768-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:23:08.857768-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-g44u.1","title":"Molecule composition: Includes directive","description":"Add support for molecule composition via the Includes directive.

## Format
\`\`\`markdown
## Molecule: derived-name
Includes: mol-base-molecule

## Step: additional-step
Additional instructions here.
Needs: step-from-base
\`\`\`

## Implementation
1. Add \`Includes:\` parsing to ParseMoleculeSteps()
2. Resolve included molecule by ID
3. Merge steps from included molecule
4. Allow new steps to depend on included steps
5. Support multiple includes (polymers)

## Files to modify
- internal/beads/molecule.go
- internal/beads/molecule_test.go

## Acceptance
- [ ] Parse Includes directive
- [ ] Resolve and merge included steps
- [ ] Dependencies across molecules work
- [ ] Multiple includes supported
- [ ] Tests cover composition scenarios","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.981634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:55.055353-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:03:55.055353-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-g44u.1","depends_on_id":"gt-g44u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T15:50:08.983662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-l9g","title":"Beads epic templates for batch work patterns","description":"Optional: Define templates for common batch work patterns.

## Concept

A template encodes a workflow pattern that can be instantiated as beads:

```yaml
# templates/batch-basic.yaml
name: basic-batch
description: Simple batch work pattern
phases:
  - name: startup
    issues:
      - title: "Verify workers ready"
  - name: working
    # Actual work issues added separately
  - name: cleanup
    issues:
      - title: "Merge all branches"
      - title: "Clean up workers"
      - title: "Report to Mayor"
```

## Usage

```bash
gt spawn --template basic-batch --epic gt-u1j --workers 3
```

Creates beads epic with template phases + actual work from gt-u1j children.

## Decision Point

Templates are OPTIONAL. The core design (beads as state, multi-wave orchestration) works without templates. Templates are sugar for common patterns.

Consider deferring to P3 or dropping entirely if beads epics with dependencies suffice.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" involved - templates just pre-populate epic/issue structure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:24.399235-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:08.868396-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-mxyj","title":"Witness session startup (gt witness start)","description":"Implement gt witness start <rig>

Should:
1. Verify rig exists and has witness/ directory
2. Check for existing witness session (don't double-start)
3. Create tmux session: gt-<rig>-witness
4. Start Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions

6. Send startup prompt

 {"id":"gt-n7z7","title":"Bug: refinery --foreground detects parent session as already running","description":"When gt refinery start gastown runs:
1. Creates tmux session gt-gastown-refinery
2. Sends 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' into the session
3. The foreground command checks HasSession() - finds the session it's inside
4. Returns 'already running' error

The foreground mode check should either:
- Skip the tmux session check (only check PID)
- Use a different indicator that the daemon loop is running
- Pass a flag to indicate we're being called from the background starter

Workaround: Manually run 'gt refinery start gastown --foreground' from a fresh session.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:56:20.326369-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:52:11.20518-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: Added timeout warnings to handoff wait, fixed refinery foreground race condition"}
 {"id":"gt-nam3","title":"Update docs to reflect molecule-first paradigm","description":"Gas Town is fundamentally a molecule execution engine. Documentation should reflect this more clearly.

## Issues Found

### 1. gt spawn examples show molecule as optional
README.md line 116: `gt spawn --issue <id> # Start polecat on issue`
Should emphasize: polecats execute molecules, not just issues.

### 2. Architecture.md spawn examples inconsistent
Line 344 shows molecule: `gt spawn --issue gt-xyz --molecule mol-engineer-in-box`
Line 1434 shows without: `gt spawn --issue <id>`

### 3. Config vs molecule distinction not clear
outposts.yaml shows static policy - should note when molecules apply.

### 4. Operational molecules section is good but buried
Lines 430-566 cover operational molecules well. Should be more prominent.

## Updates Needed
- [ ] README: Update spawn examples to show molecule usage
- [ ] architecture.md: Ensure all spawn examples include molecules
- [ ] architecture.md: Add section on "when config vs molecule"
- [ ] architecture.md: Move operational molecules higher in document
- [ ] Add principle: "If it requires cognition, it's a molecule"
- [ ] federation-design.md: Note that policy can escalate to mol-outpost-assign

## Key Message
Gas Town doesn't spawn workers on issues. It spawns workers on molecules.
The issue is just the seed data for the molecule execution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:31.842406-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:26:21.813833-08:00","close_reason":"Updated docs with molecule-first paradigm - all spawn examples include molecules, added Config vs Molecule section with cognition principle, updated federation-design.md with policy escalation note"}

-{"id":"gt-ngu1","title":"Pinned beads should appear first in mail inbox","description":"Currently bd mail inbox sorts by priority then date, but pinned beads (handoff context) should always appear at the top.

**Current behavior:**
- Pinned beads mixed in with regular mail based on priority/date

**Expected behavior:**
- Pinned beads always first in inbox (before priority sorting)
- This enables handoff beads to be the default first item an agent sees

**Implementation:**
1. In mail.go runMailInbox(), after filtering, sort pinned beads to top
2. Within pinned and non-pinned groups, maintain priority/date sort

**Related:**
- gt-r8ej: Implement pinned beads for handoff state
- gt-8h4: Pinned Beads epic

**Acceptance criteria:**
- bd mail inbox shows pinned beads first
- Pinned beads still sorted by priority/date among themselves
- Non-pinned mail sorted normally after pinned section","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ngu1","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:31.978176-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ni6a","title":"Witness role template + CLAUDE.md","description":"Create the Witness agent's role context:

 {"id":"gt-njr","title":"Engineer session restart protocol","description":"Implement session restart flow for when the Engineer needs to split work:

1. Engineer creates subtask(s) in Beads assigned to self
2. Engineer sends handoff mail to self (🤝 HANDOFF)
3. Engineer sends restart request to Witness
4. Witness verifies:
   - Handoff mail exists in Engineer outbox/sent
   - Subtasks filed in Beads
5. Witness restarts the Refinery session (new Engineer context)

This enables "occasionally consistent, eventually convergent" work patterns.
The Refinery continues; the Engineer gets fresh context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:48.22994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:42.05363-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-njr","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:56.148564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-qsvq","title":"gt doctor: Detect and clean up orphaned sessions/processes","description":"## Problem
Orphaned tmux sessions and Claude processes accumulate over time, consuming memory.

## Discovered Orphans
- gt-deacon: Idle Claude session, not part of any rig

## Proposed Solution

### gt doctor --check (default)
Detect issues without fixing:
- Orphan sessions (not matching rig/polecat/crew/witness/refinery/mayor pattern)
- Claude processes without parent tmux session
- Tmux sessions without Claude (stuck at bash prompt)
- Polecats marked 'working' but session idle

### gt doctor --fix
Clean up detected issues:
- Kill orphan sessions
- Kill orphan Claude processes
- Optionally reset stuck polecat state

### gt gc (alternative name)
Short alias for cleanup operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects orphan sessions
- [ ] Detects orphan processes
- [ ] Safe cleanup (doesn't kill active work)
- [ ] Reports what was cleaned","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"gastown/capable","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:06:39.046959-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T07:54:51.210265-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented orphan session and process detection in gt doctor"}

 {"id":"gt-sye","title":"Mayor startup protocol prompting","description":"Add startup protocol to Mayor CLAUDE.md template.

## On Session Start

1. Check for handoff:
   town inbox | grep "Session Handoff"

2. If handoff found:
   - Read it: town read <msg-id>
   - Process pending escalations (highest priority)
   - Check status of noted swarms
   - Verify rig health matches notes
   - Continue with documented next steps

3. If no handoff:
   town status     # Overall health
   town rigs       # Each rig
   bd ready        # Work items
   town inbox      # Any messages
   Build your own picture of current state.

4. After processing handoff:
   - Archive or delete the handoff message
   - You now own the current state

## Handoff Best Practices

- Be specific: 'Toast has merge conflict in auth/middleware.go' not 'Toast is stuck'
- Include context: Why decisions are pending, what you were thinking
- Prioritize next steps: What is most urgent
- Note time-sensitive items: Anything that might have changed since handoff","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:27.915484-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:57.555724-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-sye","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.459108-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-szsq","title":"gt spawn --create should auto-add polecat if missing","description":"## Problem

`gt spawn gastown --issue gt-xxx --create` fails if no polecats exist.

## Current Behavior

```
Error: auto-select polecat: no available polecats in rig 'gastown'
```

## Expected Behavior

`--create` should:
1. Create a new polecat if none exist
2. Or create the specified polecat if `gt spawn gastown/NewName --create`

## Workaround

Must manually run `gt polecat add gastown Name` first.

## Principle

Agent UX should be forgiving - if I say spawn with --create, make it work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:52:07.75062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.859926-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.859926-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-tca","title":"Polecats should auto-cleanup after MR submission","description":"Currently polecats must manually run 'gt handoff --shutdown' after completing work. This is error-prone and leaves stale polecats around.

## Desired Flow

1. Polecat completes work
2. Polecat runs 'gt mq submit' (or similar)
3. MR is added to integration queue
4. **Polecat automatically cleans up** (no manual handoff needed)

## Implementation Options

### Option A: mq submit triggers cleanup
In 'gt mq submit':
1. Submit MR to queue
2. Automatically run cleanup (same as gt handoff --shutdown)
3. Polecat session terminates

### Option B: Refinery triggers cleanup
When Refinery picks up MR:
1. Refinery processes MR
2. Sends message to Witness: 'CLEANUP: <polecat>'
3. Witness cleans up polecat

### Option C: Molecule-driven
Define cleanup as final phase of polecat-work molecule:
1. code → test → submit-mr → cleanup

## Note
This reinforces the ephemeral model: polecats exist only for the duration of a single task.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:22:54.485456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:22:54.485456-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tca","depends_on_id":"gt-9nf","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:40:08.998908-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-th7","title":"Add agent abstraction layer to support non-Claude agents","description":"Currently Gas Town hardcodes 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' throughout the codebase for spawning agents. We should add an abstraction layer to support other AI agents (e.g., Gemini CLI, OpenAI agents, local models).

Locations that spawn Claude:
- internal/cmd/mayor.go:131
- internal/cmd/deacon.go:150  
- internal/cmd/witness.go:280
- internal/cmd/crew.go (multiple locations)
- internal/cmd/up.go:190, 229
- internal/session/manager.go:146
- internal/refinery/manager.go:207

Suggested approach:
1. Create an agent package with an interface
2. Add configuration for agent type in town/rig config
3. Replace hardcoded claude commands with agent.Spawn() calls
4. Support agents: claude, gemini, openai, local (ollama, etc.)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:11:15.931048-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:26:54.236995-08:00"}
